# trainers.gg — Copilot Instructions

Pokemon community platform for competitive players. Turborepo monorepo with Next.js 16 web app, Expo 55 mobile app, Supabase backend, and Bluesky PDS integration.

## Monorepo Structure

```
apps/web/          — Next.js 16 (React 19.2, App Router)
apps/mobile/       — Expo 55 (React Native 0.83, Tamagui)
packages/supabase/ — Supabase client, queries, migrations, edge functions
packages/validators/ — Shared Zod schemas for forms, Server Actions, edge functions
packages/utils/    — Shared utilities (formatting, countries, tiers, slug generation)
packages/tournaments/ — Tournament logic (Swiss pairings, standings, brackets)
packages/pokemon/  — Pokemon data, validation, team parsing
packages/posthog/  — Shared PostHog event name constants
packages/theme/    — Shared OKLCH color tokens for web and mobile
packages/atproto/  — AT Protocol / Bluesky utilities
tooling/           — ESLint, Prettier, Tailwind, TypeScript, test-utils configs
infra/pds/         — Self-hosted Bluesky PDS on Fly.io
infra/ngrok/       — Local dev tunnel
```

## Auto-Generated Files

`packages/supabase/src/types.ts` is auto-generated by `pnpm generate-types` from the current database schema. Never modify it manually. If types for a table are absent, the table was dropped — that is intentional, not an error.

## TypeScript Conventions

- Strict mode: `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `noImplicitOverride`
- Type-only imports: `import { type Foo }` (not `import type { Foo }`)
- No `any` — use `unknown` instead
- Unused variables: prefix with `_`
- Files: `kebab-case`. Components: `PascalCase`. Functions/vars: `camelCase`. Constants: `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE`. Zod schemas: `camelCaseSchema`

## React Compiler

React Compiler is enabled (`reactCompiler: true` in `next.config.ts`). It handles memoization automatically across all packages. Never use `useMemo`, `useCallback`, or `React.memo` — they conflict with compiler optimizations.

## Shared Packages vs App Code

Code in `packages/` must have zero framework imports — pure logic usable by web, mobile, and edge functions. Framework-specific code (React hooks, Next.js infrastructure, Expo patterns) belongs in `apps/*/src/lib/`.

## Validation

All user input validation lives in `@trainers/validators` (shared package). Schemas use Zod with a transform+pipe pattern for trimming before validation:

```ts
// Trim, then validate length
z.string()
  .transform((val) => val.trim())
  .pipe(z.string().max(100));
```

Empty optional strings are transformed to `undefined` so downstream code doesn't need to handle `""`.

## Error Handling

- `throw new Error()` for validation failures
- Return `null` from queries when not found
- Server Actions return `{ success: boolean; error?: string }`

## Styling

- The web app uses **Tailwind CSS v4** (`@tailwindcss/postcss`). Tailwind v4 has a continuous spacing scale — every integer value (e.g. `w-22`, `h-22`) is valid. Do not flag integer spacing utilities as missing; they are all generated on-demand.
- Always use `cn()` from `@/lib/utils` for dynamic Tailwind classes. Template literals cause Tailwind purge issues.

## Design System

- Minimal flat design: no borders, subtle background differentiation
- Teal primary accent (OKLCH tokens from `@trainers/theme`)
- `StatusBadge` component for semantic status colors
- Never render raw enum/DB values in UI — map to human-readable labels

## Testing

- Every feature/bugfix must include tests (CI enforces 60% patch coverage)
- Use Fishery factories from `@trainers/test-utils` — never inline object literals
- Use `it.each` for parameterized tests
- Test domain logic and decisions, not framework behavior
